text: Production HQ for The Asha Show on Rhythm FM 94.7. Rundowns, segment archive, and the ideas board all live here.
Quick links
Open Show Rundowns, Segment Archive, and the Off-Mic Launch Tasks board.
Cold open: weekend traffic stories from Melville, then warm into the breakfast belt. Energy up, keep it local.
Clock
06:10 headlines and weather, 06:40 traffic with Lerato, 07:20 listener call-ins, 08:15 sponsor read.
Confirm 08:15 sponsor copy with Palesa before air
Tuesday means Voices of the City. This week: the Brixton water crisis follow-up. Lead with the human story first.
Feature plan
Pre-recorded vox pops from residents, then live ward councillor at 07:35.
Ask Naledi to level the vox pops, some were recorded outdoors
Midweek show. Keep it light, listeners are tired by Wednesday. Plenty of music, shorter talk breaks.
Clock
07:10 What's On in Joburg this weekend, 08:30 throwback track of the day.
Pull three throwback options from the Music Rotation list
Aired segment with Mandla Sithole on the Soweto jazz revival. Gorgeous tape, he brought his trumpet in studio.
Notes
Best clip at 04:12, the bit about his grandfather's shebeen sessions. Use for socials.
Send Mandla a thank-you and the clip link
Reverend Grace Moyo on the street kitchens feeding Melville through winter. Warm, generous interview.
Notes
She gave a great call to action for donations, flag for a follow-up next month.
Archive the master to the Segment Archive
Idea: invite listeners to send a short love letter to Joburg. Read the best ones on air on Fridays.
Why it works
Low cost, high warmth, gives the breakfast show a recurring emotional beat. Could feed the book too.
Pitch the segment to Dr. Khumalo at next planning meeting
Idea: a three-part mini-series capturing the soundscapes of Joburg taxi ranks. Could be an Off-Mic special.
Next steps
Scout Bree Street and Noord ranks with Kagiso for field recording.
Check field recorder availability with Naledi
Potential guest: Nandi Dlamini, the young poet laureate. Brilliant on youth and the city. Great fit for VOTC.
Booking status
Palesa reaching out via her agent. Aim for a Tuesday slot in July.
Confirm available July Tuesdays with Nandi
Off-Mic launches 30 Sep 2026. Landing page copy needs to sound like me, not corporate. Conversational, a bit cheeky.
Open tasks
Write hero headline and the two pilot teasers
Get Themba's sign-off on the Kgosi Media branding
Both pilots need a final master with Naledi before we cut trailers. Target loudness for podcast platforms.
Checklist
Book Naledi for a half-day mastering session at Studio B
Voices of the City S3 follow-up on the Brixton water crisis. We promised listeners we would track the council's response.
Angle
Get the latest from Johannesburg Water on the repair timeline
Book chapter 4 draft on the pirate radio days of SA broadcasting. Due with the full proposal by 31 Dec 2026.
Beats to cover
Interview two veteran DJs from the 1980s underground stations
Rhythm FM 30th Anniversary doc. Sipho is co-directing the archive segment. Shoot window is November 2026.
Shot list
Pull station tape from 1996 launch from the Kgosi Media archive
JMU Youth Media Mentorship winter cohort. Intro session covers mic technique and basic storytelling.
Agenda
Bring spare headphones and the portable recorder for the students
Sponsor read for the Joburg City Library winter reading drive. 30 seconds, runs at 08:15 all week.
Copy
Get final wording approved by Palesa before Monday's show
